source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools—once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals—and makes them accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability.




THE ROLE

Great design has always been one of Arta's defining strengths. As we expand into enterprise software and AI-powered platforms, maintaining that level of craftsmanship while designing for greater complexity is one of our biggest competitive advantages.

We're looking for a senior designer who doesn't stop at a design tool. You'll build real, working prototypes that engineering can react to and build from, and you'll design systems that hold together whether they're being used by our internal team, shown inside a partner's app, or given to a private bank's advisors.

You'll work at the intersection of AI, wealth management, and enterprise software, partnering closely with product and engineering to turn complex problems into experiences that are elegant, intuitive, and production-ready. We care deeply about craft, and we're looking for someone who shares that obsession with quality.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Design AI-native workflows, including conversational interfaces and human-in-the-loop interactions, that help users complete complex financial tasks with confidence

 - Shape product direction with Product and Engineering by turning customer problems into intuitive experiences, working collaboratively to define what should be built, not just how it should look.

 - Prototype and validate ideas using modern design and AI-assisted tools, testing with real users before engineering builds

 - Build and evolve a design system flexible enough to support customization without breaking visual and interaction consistency

 - Raise the bar for craft across every interaction, from typography and motion to micro-interactions and visual polish

 - Partner directly with engineering, product, and partner-facing teams, including external partner design and brand teams where relevant.

 - Work closely with engineers through implementation to ensure shipped products match the intended user experience and maintain a high standard of quality.



WHO YOU ARE

 - 6+ years of in-house product design experience, spanning consumer and/or platform products

 - A portfolio demonstrating strong product thinking, interaction design, visual craft, and interactive prototypes that closely resemble production experiences

 - Demonstrated ability to simplify highly complex workflows into elegant, intuitive user experiences

 - Experience designing and building platforms



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience simplifying complex or information-dense workflows; fintech or enterprise software experience is a plus

 - Experience managing executive stakeholders, internal and external

 - Comfortable using customer research, analytics, experimentation, and qualitative feedback to inform design decisions

Important: Please submit your portfolio with the application




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Robust health insurance offering for you and your family

 - 20 weeks of parental leave

 - 15 days PTO annually plus national and company holidays

 - Up to SG$75 per month reimbursement for internet

source_jobs.title: Full-Stack / Frontend Agentic Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools to make financial products once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability.




THE ROLE

In this role, you will own user-facing features end-to-end — with a primary focus on the frontend and the ability to drop into the backend when a feature requires it. You'll design and build the interfaces that make sophisticated wealth management feel effortless, while leveraging AI coding agents and modern tooling to iterate faster and ship higher-craft work than a traditional frontend team.

You'll partner closely with designers, product managers, and backend engineers to deliver polished, trustworthy experiences for Arta's customers — and you'll own enough of the stack to ship a full slice when needed.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Build elegant, responsive, accessible web interfaces using modern frontend frameworks — this is the core of your work

 - Translate Figma designs and product specs into production-quality UI, with strong attention to motion, interaction, and detail

 - Use AI coding agents (Claude Code, v0, Cursor, etc.) as a core part of your workflow — for prototyping, iterating on UI, generating component variants, and accelerating delivery

 - Prototype quickly to explore design directions and pressure-test ideas before committing to production builds

 - Build agent- and LLM-powered UI patterns (conversational interfaces, AI-assisted flows, intelligent forms) that thoughtfully bring AI into the user experience

 - Own the frontend craft bar: performance, accessibility, design system consistency, and cross-browser/device quality

 - Contribute to backend APIs, data models, and service code when a feature requires it — owning the full slice end-to-end

 - Partner with backend engineers on API design from the consumer's perspective

 - Contribute to the design system and reusable component libraries

 - Investigate production issues and participate in incident response as needed




WHO YOU ARE

 - At least 6 years of software engineering experience, with the majority focused on frontend — deep proficiency in React (or similar modern framework) and TypeScript

 - Strong design sensibility — you care about typography, spacing, motion, and interaction quality, and can collaborate as a peer with designers

 - Experience with design systems, component libraries, and modern CSS approaches (Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, etc.)

 - Comfortable working in the backend when needed — you can write production-quality API endpoints, data models, and service code in Python (or similar), even if it isn't your primary craft

 - Solid understanding of RESTful APIs, databases (SQL and NoSQL), and how frontend and backend systems fit together

 - Fluency with AI coding tools and a track record of using them to ship higher-craft UI work faster

 - Experience or strong interest in building AI-native UI patterns (chat, streaming, tool-use surfaces, agent UIs)

 - Experience with version control (Git) and collaborative development workflows

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - Prior experience in fintech, trading, or financial product UX is a big plus, but not a must




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro call with the Head of Talent, 30m

 2. Technical Interview 1: Coding (in Python)/Algorithm/Data Structure, 60m
    
    1. Leet code questions using CoderPad
    
    2. This round is conducted virtually on Google Meet

 3. Technical Interview 2: System Design,

source_jobs.title: Backend / Systems Agentic Engineer (SG)
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools to make financial products once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability.




THE ROLE

In this role, you will design and build the backend systems and infrastructure that power Arta's mission-critical financial services. You'll own services end-to-end, from API design to data models to production reliability, while working extensively with AI coding agents and LLM-based tooling to ship faster and operate at higher leverage than a traditional backend team.

You'll collaborate with product managers, frontend engineers, and ML engineers to deliver resilient backend services that handle real money, real data, and real users.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Design, develop, and maintain backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that power Arta's investment and wealth management platform

 - Make architectural decisions on system design, data modeling, service boundaries, and infrastructure

 - Write clean, well-tested, observable code, and use AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) as a core part of your daily workflow to accelerate delivery without sacrificing quality

 - Build and integrate LLM and agent-powered backend capabilities where they unlock product value (e.g., automated workflows, intelligent data processing, agent-driven user experiences)

 - Optimize for performance, scalability, and reliability across distributed services

 - Integrate with third-party financial APIs, brokerage systems, market data providers, and cloud infrastructure

 - Set up logging, monitoring, and alerting (Sentry, Datadog, etc.) and participate in on-call and incident response

 - Drive engineering best practices around code review, testing, and evaluation, including evals for any AI/agent-driven systems you ship

 - Partner with product and design to translate ambiguous problems into well-scoped backend solutions

 - Design and build reliable agent orchestration workflows using Temporal, leveraging state machines and workflow orchestration patterns to manage complex, long-running backend processes.




WHO YOU ARE

 - At least 6 years of backend software development experience, with strong proficiency in Python (or similar)

 - Deep understanding of distributed systems, RESTful and/or gRPC APIs, microservices, and database systems (SQL and NoSQL)

 - Experience designing for reliability, performance, and security in production environments

 - Fluency with AI coding tools and a track record of using them to materially raise your output and code quality

 - Experience building with LLMs and/or agent frameworks (tool use, function calling, MCP, evals) or strong interest and aptitude to ramp up quickly

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills

 - Prior experience in trading platforms, brokerage systems, or financial infrastructure is a big plus, but not a must




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Intro call with the Head of Talent, 30m

 2. Technical Interview 1: Coding (in Python)/Algorithm/Data Structure, 60m
    
    1. Leet code questions using CoderPad
    
    2. This round is conducted virtually on Google Meet

 3. Technical Interview 2: System Design, 60m

 4. Technical Interview 3: AI-augmented Interview, 60m
    
    1. Details will be briefed by the Head of Talent

 5. Culture Interview with the Chief of Staff, 30m




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Robust health insurance offering for you and your family

 - 20 weeks of parental leave

 - 15 days PTO annually

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Private Investments Platform (Alternatives)
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools—once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals—and makes them accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability. Think: intelligent investing, personalized portfolios, and real-time trading, all backed by robust data infrastructure.





THE ROLE

Arta is building an AI-native wealth platform that expands access to sophisticated investment strategies — including private markets such as private equity, venture capital, private credit, and other alternative assets.

We are looking for a senior software engineer to work closely with the Private Investments Team to build and scale our Alternatives platform and go-to-market capabilities.

This role sits at the intersection of:

 - software engineering

 - private markets / alternative investments

 - product and platform design for institutional and high-net-worth clients

You won’t just be handed specs — you’ll help define how alternative investments are structured, delivered, and scaled through software.

If you’re excited about building products that bring traditionally opaque, manual asset classes into modern, scalable platforms — this will be a strong fit.




WHAT YOU WILL DO




BUILD THE ALTERNATIVES PLATFORM

 - Design, build, and own end-to-end backend systems powering the alternatives platform, working closely with product and business stakeholders to develop, ship, and iterate on features across the full lifecycle of private market investments—from fund onboarding and deal execution to capital calls and distributions.

 - Architect scalable data models and services to support complex fund structures and investor allocations, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and extensibility across the platform.

 - Develop robust APIs, workflows, and internal tooling that enable reliable tracking of performance, cash flows, and portfolio exposure across alternative assets, while supporting operational efficiency for internal teams and seamless integration with user-facing applications.


DRIVE GO-TO-MARKET ENABLEMENT

 - Partner directly with Private Investments Team to translate investment strategies into scalable product experiences

 - Build internal and client-facing tools that support fund launches, client onboarding, and investor reporting

 - Enable operational workflows that are traditionally manual (subscription docs, diligence workflows, reporting)


BRIDGE INVESTMENTS AND ENGINEERING

 - Work cross-functionally with investment, product, legal, and operations teams

 - Translate ambiguous, domain-heavy requirements into clean system designs

 - Bring structure and scalability to complex financial processes


BUILD FOR SCALE AND RELIABILITY

 - Architect backend systems and APIs for internal and external use

 - Ensure data accuracy, auditability, and consistency across financial workflows

 - Contribute to overall platform architecture and engineering standards




WHO YOU ARE

 - 7–10 years of experience as a software engineer with strong command in Python

 - Strong backend / systems engineering fundamentals

 - Experience building production systems with real-world complexity (data models, workflows, APIs)

 - Basic familiarity with private markets / alternative investments, such as:
   
   - Private equity / venture capital
   
   - Private credit
   
   - Fund structures, capital calls, distributions

 - Ability to work in ambiguous environments and partner closely with non-engineering stakeholders

 - Strong product sense — you care about how systems translate into user experience

 - Based in the Bay area or you have plans to rel

source_jobs.title: Director, Client Advisory
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

At Arta Finance, we are product builders, machine learning researchers, finance professionals, and business leaders determined to create a better way to manage our financial lives. The future of finance is technology—relentlessly user-focused, elegant, and bordering-on-magical. We apply deep tech to help people enjoy more successful financial lives—and we believe we can have fun doing it.

If you’ve ever wondered whether your money is working as hard as you are, or thought there must be a better way to pursue your financial goals, we want to hear from you.

 


THE ROLE

We are looking for a client-centered Director of Client Advisory to join our growing advisory team. You’ll support our U.S. clients in developing and executing sophisticated financial plans and constructing portfolios across multiple asset classes. You will partner directly with members to understand their goals, design tailored strategies, manage implementation, and monitor performance. You will partner closely with other product experts to design the right portfolio for members.

 


WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Develop tailored financial plans and multi-asset portfolio construction strategies aligned with client objectives

 - Systematize model portfolios and financial planning workflows in cooperation with engineering and product teams to provide a scalable solution to Arta's external partners

 - Manage clients’ portfolios, including implementation and execution of investment strategies, ongoing monitoring, periodic reviews, and adjustments as needed to meet plan goals.

 - Educate clients on investment options, asset allocation, portfolio risks, performance, and market outlooks. Develop market and investment content.

 - Stay current with capital markets trends, economic developments, regulatory changes, investment product innovations, and competitor offerings to provide informed recommendations.

 - Ensure all advisory activities comply with relevant regulatory requirements and uphold fiduciary standards

 - Collaborate with internal stakeholders (operations, compliance, product, research) to support seamless service delivery, streamline processes, and enhance client experience.

 - Product Knowledge and Training: Have a deep understanding of Arta’s offering and tools, while staying up to date on our training


WHO YOU ARE

 - Minimum 10 years of experience delivering comprehensive financial plans, including retirement planning, wealth accumulation strategies, tax-aware investing, and estate considerations. Knowledge of life insurance and annuities is a plus.

 - Hold the necessary securities qualifications for Investment Adviser Representative (IAR) registration — such as Series 65, Series 7 + Series 66, or an approved designation like CFP®, CFA®, or ChFC®. Certifications like CFP or CAIA are a plus.

 - You are highly hands-on and thrive in a fast-paced environment where wearing multiple hats is the norm. You know when to roll up your sleeves and solve problems directly, and when to leverage the expertise of others. Above all, you are committed to delivering exceptional, end-to-end outcomes for clients and ensuring a best-in-class wealth management experience.

Experience partnering cross-functionally to develop scalable investment planning frameworks; comfortable serving as a subject matter expert to external partners and translating sophisticated advisory methodologies into repeatable, automated workflows.

 - Have no disqualifying criminal, regulatory, or securities-related history.

 - Analytical mindset with the ability to take market, economic, and financial data and translate it into actionable recommendations.

 - Strong ethical judgment, professionalism, and commitment to acting in a fiduciary capacity. Strong understanding of portfolio construction, asset allocation, risk management, and multi-asset investment strategies.

 - Ability to create strong relationships and trust through an online remote environment

 

Arta's Compensation Ph

source_jobs.title: Associate, US Operations - Digital Wealth Platform (Bay area and remote)
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools—once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals—and makes them accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability. Think: intelligent investing, personalized portfolios, and real-time trading, all backed by robust data infrastructure.




THE ROLE

As an Operations Associate, you will play a key role in building a culture of operational discipline within Arta by managing the core operational processes which power the end-to-end user journey of our clients. This includes client onboarding, deposits/withdrawals, trading across all our asset classes and monitoring of account activity. In doing so, you will collaborate with both internal (Customer Success, Product, Compliance, Engineering) and external (broker-dealers, fund managers) stakeholders.



The ideal candidate should be an advocate for the user experience of our customers, have an innate curiosity to understand how things work behind-the-scenes and a strong drive for improving processes.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own and execute the critical operational processes that power our platform, in the areas of public and private market investments (e.g. buy/sell trades, reconciliation, corporate actions, capital calls, NAV updates), customer onboarding and risk assessment, account management, and money transfers.

 - Collaborate with our Operations, Member Success, Product, Compliance and Engineering teams to:
   
   - Anticipate future business needs;
   
   - Brainstorm workflows and edge cases to operationalize new products; and
   
   - Enhance key processes while considering implications such as cost, implementation complexity, regulatory compliance and other relevant risks.

 - Coordinate the efforts of internal and external partners to investigate operational issues and implement preventive measures.

 - Create data reporting workflows to track key operational metrics and manage regulatory reporting obligations.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 3-4+ years of business operations experience in financial services, ideally in the investments space handling a mix of asset classes.

 - Have experience designing and implementing end-to-end operational processes with appropriate controls.

 - Technical problem-solving skills: Enjoy diving into root causes and breaking down complex problems

 - You thrive in a startup-like environment where not everything is black and white. You are comfortable adapting to evolving priorities, navigating ambiguous and complex problem spaces, running experiments, and staying optimistic through change.

 - Strong attention to detail and ability to work independently.

 - Bonus: Proficiency with SQL, Excel and setting up AI workflows.

 - Location: Ideally you are based in the Bay Area but we are open to remote candidates if you have strong experience. (Ideally, west-coast time zone)




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Hiring Manager interview with Global Head of Operations based in Singapore, 45m

 2. Cross-functional interview with Global Head of Customer Success and/or Customer Success Manager, 45m

 3. Cross-functional interview with Global Head of Structured Products, 30m

 4. Co-founder/Culture interview with General Counsel, 30m




WHAT WE OFFER

 - A competitive salary and benefits package, with ample opportunities for growth and advancement

 - A vibrant and dynamic work environment where innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning are highly valued

 - The opportunity to work with a diverse and talented team of industry experts, passionate about shaping the future of finance

 - Hybrid work environment with micro-ki

source_jobs.title: Relationship Manager (Contract-to-Hire) - Singapore
source_jobs.description:
THE ROLE

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. We are growing our team with an entrepreneurial Relationship Manager who wants to build and own a book of high-net-worth client relationships from the ground up. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability.



This is a sales-oriented seat for a hunter: someone who can originate new client relationships, convert opportunities, and own the follow-through required to build trust over time. You will join on a fixed-term contract designed as a runway to a permanent role: a defined window to prove you can originate, convert, and grow client relationships on the Arta platform, with a clear path to converting to a full-time seat - and materially greater upside - once you do. If you have built a book before (or want your shot to) and want to build on a platform that gives you better products and far greater reach than a traditional private or priority bank, let's chat.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Originate, convert, and grow a standalone book of high-net-worth client relationships on the Arta platform

 - Pitch differentiated structured products, alternatives, and cross-asset solutions to clients in a way that is understandable, suitable, and in their best interest

 - Own the full client lifecycle - from first conversation through funding, transacting, and deepening wallet share over time

 - Activate, develop, and manage a pipeline of prospects, working closely with your manager on strategy while operating independently in client-facing situations

 - Represent Arta and its brand to the market with judgement and care

 - Partner with Compliance, Operations, and Product to ensure every client interaction meets Arta's standards

 - Feed the front line back into the platform - what clients want, what's working, and where we can be better




WHO YOU ARE

 - You are a builder and a closer, motivated by developing long-term client relationships and turning them into meaningful commercial outcomes.

 - You have 2-5 years of experience supporting, advising, or originating client relationships at a private bank, brokerage, EAM, or comparable institution.

 - You are comfortable discussing public market solutions, structured products and private market offerings with sophisticated clients.

 - You are highly commercial, with outstanding verbal and written communication skills and a high bar for client experience

 - You hold, or can promptly obtain, the relevant MAS qualifications required to act in a client-facing wealth advisory role in Singapore, including CACS.

 - You are comfortable operating with ambiguity and autonomy in an early-stage environment, and motivated by a performance-linked path.

 - You are based in Singapore and have the necessary right to work in Singapore for the duration of the contract engagement.




STRUCTURE AND WHAT WE OFFER

 - A fixed-term contract engagement designed as a pathway to a permanent Relationship Manager role

 - Candidates who successfully convert to a permanent role will be eligible for discretionary performance-based compensation and significantly greater long-term earning potential

 - Differentiated products, modern AI-enabled tooling, and the support of Arta's investment, structuring, and operations teams

 - The opportunity to build - and own - your own book of business within Arta’s fast-growing Singapore team.



Arta determines level and compensation based on factors including interview performance, job-related skills, experience, and relevant qualifications. Final terms, including the structure of the contract period and the permanent conversion package, are discussed directly with candidates during the process.

source_jobs.title: VP of Product, B2B Wealth Platform
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools—once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals—and makes them accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability. Think: intelligent investing, personalized portfolios, and real-time trading, all backed by robust data infrastructure.




THE ROLE

Arta Finance is building an AI-powered wealth platform that brings sophisticated investment capabilities and portfolio intelligence to modern financial institutions. Our platform enables banks, digital banks, and financial platforms to deliver institutional-grade investment products and portfolio insights to their clients through modern infrastructure and APIs.

We are looking for a strategic and execution-oriented VP of Product Management to lead the evolution of Arta’s institutional wealth platform globally. This leader will define the long-term product vision and platform strategy for Arta’s B2B wealth infrastructure while driving execution across partner deployments, platform capabilities, and investment workflows.

This role is ideal for someone with an engineering background or who has been in the tech industry for a long time, and who enjoys working closely with technical teams to build scalable financial platforms.

You will own the strategy and execution of core platform capabilities used by Arta’s global institutional partners, reporting to the Singapore CEO, and partnering closely with VP of Engineering and the Chief of Staff to define the roadmap and drive execution.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Own the B2B Wealth Platform Roadmap

 - Define the strategic direction for Arta’s institutional wealth platform and partner ecosystem, balancing long-term platform vision with near-term partner and market needs

 - Identify emerging opportunities in wealth infrastructure, AI-enabled advisory, and institutional investing to shape Arta’s next generation platform capabilities

 - Build scalable capabilities that power investment products, portfolio analytics, and advisor tools.
   Ensure the platform can support multiple partners through modular APIs and services.

Translate Complex Investment Workflows Into Software

 - Design systems supporting workflows such as:
   
   - portfolio construction and analytics
   
   - investment product distribution
   
   - portfolio reporting and performance tracking
   
   - trading and order workflows
   
   - advisor and client dashboards

 - Work closely with engineering and UI/UX teams to design scalable, reliable platform architecture.

Build Developer-Friendly Platform Capabilities

 - Lead the development of API-driven infrastructure used by partner institutions.

 - Ensure Arta’s platform can integrate easily with partner systems such as digital banking apps, core banking systems, and wealth platforms.

Partner Closely With Engineering and AI Teams

 - Collaborate with engineers and AI researchers to design and ship complex technical products.

 - Translate business and partner needs into clear product specifications.

 - Provide strategic product leadership in architectural and platform design decisions, ensuring technical investments align with long-term business, scalability, and partner objectives.

Work With Institutional Partners

 - Serve as a senior product leader and strategic counterpart to institutional partners, helping shape joint platform initiatives, integration strategies, and long-term product evolution.

 - Balance partner customization with the long-term scalability of the platform.

Drive Product Execution

 - Drive cross-functional execution across product, engineering, design, AI, and

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer, AI Agent Platform
source_jobs.description:
THE COMPANY

Arta is on an audacious and incredibly rewarding mission: to pave the way for people everywhere to lead more successful financial lives. Arta leverages AI and sophisticated digital tools—once reserved for ultra-high-net-worth individuals—and makes them accessible to a broader global audience. Think of it as your own digital family office, combining intelligent investment strategies, alternative assets, private market access, and smart automation to help you grow and protect your wealth effortlessly. We value trust, teamwork, and adaptability. Think: intelligent investing, personalized portfolios, and real-time trading, all backed by robust data infrastructure.




THE ROLE

Arta is building the AI infrastructure for the next generation of wealth management.

We partner with leading financial institutions to power strategic initiatives that create real competitive advantage, particularly in making high-quality, personalised advice scalable.

Our platform enables intelligent agents to operate across core advisory workflows, from client servicing and suitability to portfolio research and analysis. These systems run in live, regulated environments and are embedded into how institutions serve their clients day to day.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

You will design and build production-ready agent systems that sit at the core of how financial decisions are supported and delivered.

Build the AI Agent Platform

 - Design and implement agent architectures (tool use, planning, memory, orchestration)

 - Build systems for LLM orchestration, prompt management, and workflow execution

 - Develop evaluation frameworks for agent quality, reliability, and safety

 - Create benchmarking pipelines to measure model and system performance over time

Enable Enterprise Deployment

 - Build infrastructure for self-hosted and multi-tenant deployments

 - Design systems that operate under enterprise constraints (security, latency, cost)

 - Develop APIs and platform abstractions for external partners

Bridge Research → Production

 - Translate rapidly evolving LLM capabilities into stable, production-ready systems

 - Partner with ML and product teams to integrate agents into real financial workflows

 - Improve reliability, observability, and failure handling of agent systems




WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years building production ML systems or backend systems for ML-powered products

 - Hands-on experience with LLMs, agent frameworks, or applied ML systems

 - Strong Python skills and experience with modern ML tooling

 - Experience with agent systems, tool use, or LLM orchestration frameworks

 - Experience building evaluation / benchmarking systems for ML or LLMs

 - Experience designing systems beyond notebooks — APIs, services, pipelines

 - Strong systems thinking: latency, reliability, failure modes, tradeoffs

 - Location: You are located in or have a plan to relocate to the Bay area.


STRONG PLUS

 - Experience with self-hosted models or enterprise AI deployments

 - Background in distributed systems or data infrastructure

 - Exposure to financial systems or high-stakes domains




WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DIFFERENT

 - This is not a research or prototype-focused role.

 - You will be responsible for shipping systems that operate in live financial environments. Your work directly supports institutional clients and real end users at some of the largest and fastest-growing financial institutions, not internal demos.

 - If you’re motivated by making agent systems work reliably at scale, in complex and regulated settings, this role will be a strong fit.




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Introduction with Head of Talent, 30m

 2. General & Domain Knowledge Interview with AI Researcher, 45m

 3. Coding/Algorithm/Data Structures, 60m

 4. AI Coding & Discussion Exercise with AI Researcher, 120m

 5. System Design Interview with VP of Engineering, 60m

 6. Co-founder Interview with Head of AI/CIO, 30m


NOTE: WE REQUIRE AT LEAST ONE IN-PERSON INTERVIEW BEFORE

notes: Arta Finance is a fintech company with a strong focus on AI and digital tools. The job postings suggest a product-heavy organization with a mix of technical and non-technical roles.
